        id:5487 paragraph
          id:125 StaticText The American singer-songwriter 
          id:5488 link Taylor Swift
            id:126 StaticText Taylor Swift
          id:127 StaticText  has released 12 original albums, 4 
          id:5489 link re-recorded albums
            id:128 StaticText re-recorded albums
          id:129 StaticText , 5 
          id:5490 link extended plays (EPs)
            id:130 StaticText extended plays (EPs)
          id:131 StaticText , and 4 
          id:5491 link live albums
            id:132 StaticText live albums
          id:133 StaticText . In the United States, as of May 2025
          id:134 StaticText , she had sold 116.7 million 
          id:5494 link album-equivalent units
            id:135 StaticText album-equivalent units
          id:136 StaticText , coming from 54 million pure sales and 70.7 billion 
          id:5495 link streams
            id:137 StaticText streams
          id:138 StaticText ;
          id:5496 superscript
            id:5497 link [1]
              id:139 StaticText [
              id:140 StaticText 1
              id:141 StaticText ]
          id:142 StaticText  the 
          id:5500 link Recording Industry Association of America
            id:143 StaticText Recording Industry Association of America
          id:144 StaticText  (RIAA) as of November 2025
          id:5503 link certified
            id:146 StaticText certified
          id:147 StaticText  110 million album units, making her the 
          id:5504 link highest-certified female album artist
            id:148 StaticText highest-certified female album artist
          id:149 StaticText .

          id:153 StaticText  On the 
          id:5509 link Billboard 200
            id:154 StaticText Billboard
            id:155 StaticText  200
          id:156 StaticText  chart, she has accumulated 15 albums and 96 total weeks at number one, more than any other solo act.

        id:5515 paragraph
          id:160 StaticText Swift released six albums with 
          id:5516 link Big Machine Records
            id:161 StaticText Big Machine Records
          id:162 StaticText . Her debut album, 
          id:5517 link Taylor Swift
            id:163 StaticText Taylor Swift
          id:164 StaticText  (2006), spent more weeks on the 
          id:165 StaticText Billboard
          id:166 StaticText  200 than any other album in the 2000s decade,
          id:5520 superscript
            id:5521 link [4]
              id:167 StaticText [
              id:168 StaticText 4
              id:169 StaticText ]
          id:170 StaticText  and her second, 
          id:5525 link Fearless
            id:171 StaticText Fearless
          id:172 StaticText  (2008), was the only album from the 2000s decade to spend one year in the top 10 of the 
          id:173 StaticText Billboard
          id:174 StaticText  200.
          id:5527 superscript
            id:5528 link [5]
              id:175 StaticText [
              id:176 StaticText 5
              id:177 StaticText ]
          id:11287 StaticText  
          id:178 StaticText Fearless
          id:179 StaticText  was Swift's first album to top the charts in Australia, Canada, and New Zealand.
          id:5532 superscript

              id:182 StaticText ]
          id:183 StaticText  Her next albums—
          id:5537 link Speak Now
            id:184 StaticText Speak Now
          id:185 StaticText  (2010), 
          id:5539 link Red
            id:186 StaticText Red
          id:187 StaticText  (2012), 
          id:5541 link 1989
            id:188 StaticText 1989
          id:189 StaticText  (2014), and 
          id:5542 link Reputation
            id:190 StaticText Reputation
          id:191 StaticText  (2017)—all reached number one on the 
          id:192 StaticText Billboard
          id:193 StaticText  200, and each of them sold over one million US first-week copies.

        id:5643 paragraph
          id:296 StaticText Swift's departure from Big Machine resulted in a 
          id:5644 link dispute over the ownership of her first six albums
            id:297 StaticText dispute over the ownership of her first six albums
          id:298 StaticText  in 2019, which resulted in her re-recording four albums—
          id:5646 link Fearless (Taylor's Version)
            id:299 StaticText Fearless (Taylor's Version)
          id:300 StaticText  (2021), 
          id:5648 link Red (Taylor's Version)
            id:301 StaticText Red (Taylor's Version)
          id:302 StaticText  (2021), 
          id:5650 link Speak Now (Taylor's Version)
            id:303 StaticText Speak Now (Taylor's Version)
          id:304 StaticText  (2023), and 
          id:5652 link 1989 (Taylor's Version)
            id:305 StaticText 1989 (Taylor's Version)
          id:306 StaticText  (2023)—all of which reached number one in Australia, Canada,
          id:5653 superscript
            id:5654 link [8]
              id:307 StaticText [

              id:309 StaticText ]
          id:310 StaticText  Ireland,
